title: aquilegia coerulea subsp. daileyae
intro: Aquilegia coerulea subsp. daileyae, also known as Dailey's blue columbine, is a subspecies of the Aquilegia coerulea plant, which is a flowering plant native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, making it effective in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Dailey's blue columbine has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and digestive problems, due to its ability to soothe and calm the body. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it easily accessible for those seeking its health benefits.
